exercises.title: Speedup Calculation
exercises.description: Calculate the speedup Grover's algorithm provides for various database sizes.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def grover_speedup(N):
    """
    Calculate classical queries, quantum queries, and speedup factor.
    
    Returns: (classical_queries, quantum_queries, speedup)
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test with various sizes
for N in [64, 256, 1024, 4096]:
    classical, quantum, speedup = grover_speedup(N)
    print(f"N={N}: Classical={classical}, Quantum={quantum}, Speedup={speedup}x")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def grover_speedup(N):
    """
    Calculate classical queries, quantum queries, and speedup factor.
    """
    classical_queries = N
    quantum_queries = int(np.ceil(np.pi/4 * np.sqrt(N)))
    speedup = classical_queries // quantum_queries
    return (classical_queries, quantum_queries, speedup)

# Test with various sizes
for N in [64, 256, 1024, 4096]:
    classical, quantum, speedup = grover_speedup(N)
    print(f"N={N}: Classical={classical}, Quantum={quantum}, Speedup={speedup}x")

exercises.title: Cryptographic Impact
exercises.description: Calculate the effective security level of symmetric encryption keys under Grover's attack.
exercises.starterCode:
def effective_security(key_bits):
    """
    Calculate effective security bits after Grover's attack.
    
    Grover halves the effective key length.
    """
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test
print("Key Security Under Grover's Attack:")
print(f"{'Original':>10} {'Effective':>10} {'Recommendation':>20}")
print("-" * 45)

for bits in [56, 128, 192, 256]:
    effective = effective_security(bits)
    recommend = "UNSAFE" if effective < 80 else "OK" if effective < 128 else "SECURE"
    print(f"{bits:>10} {effective:>10} {recommend:>20}")
exercises.solution:
def effective_security(key_bits):
    """
    Calculate effective security bits after Grover's attack.
    """
    return key_bits // 2

print("Key Security Under Grover's Attack:")
print(f"{'Original':>10} {'Effective':>10} {'Recommendation':>20}")
print("-" * 45)

for bits in [56, 128, 192, 256]:
    effective = effective_security(bits)
    recommend = "UNSAFE" if effective < 80 else "OK" if effective < 128 else "SECURE"
    print(f"{bits:>10} {effective:>10} {recommend:>20}")
hints: 128-bit security is the minimum recommended today
